title: China-to-Nicaragua Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000 Series Evidence for Nicaragua PV Inverter Projects
rows.target_requirement: No publicly confirmed Nicaragua-specific mandatory EMC technical regulation for PV inverters has been identified in the public sources reviewed for this draft. For grid-tied PV projects, EMC evidence should therefore be prepared around internationally recognized IEC documentation and any NTON/DGCN, MEM/INE, ENATREL, distributor, lender, or project-owner requirement that applies to the specific procurement. IEC 62920 is the dedicated EMC standard for photovoltaic power conversion equipment, and IEC 61000-series standards are commonly used for harmonic emissions, immunity, and power-quality evidence. Because Nicaragua operates at 60 Hz, EMC and harmonic data obtained only under China's 50 Hz test conditions should be reviewed for retesting or engineering equivalence before submission.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility standards, including harmonic-current emission and immunity methods where applicable
Nicaraguan NTON/DGCN conformity context where a national technical standard or project specification requires it
MEM / INE / ENATREL / distributor project requirements where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is commonly embedded in GB/T 37408 testing and domestic certification files issued by CNAS-accredited laboratories. Those reports may use IEC 61000-derived methods, but they are issued under a Chinese domestic standard scope and commonly under 50 Hz supply conditions. They should not be presented as automatic IEC 62920 or Nicaragua project EMC acceptance unless the report explicitly maps to the IEC clause set, test configuration, power rating, firmware configuration, and 60 Hz operating condition required by the project.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related requirements
China CNAS-accredited domestic laboratory reports under Chinese GB/T scope
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T 37408 EMC evidence is not enough by itself for a Nicaragua PV project if the procurement, lender, MEM/INE reviewer, ENATREL, distributor, or NTON/DGCN conformity path expects IEC evidence. Exporters should prepare IEC 62920 EMC type-test evidence for the specific inverter model, applicable IEC 61000 harmonic and immunity reports, a test-condition statement covering 60 Hz operation, and a clause-by-clause cross-reference explaining any Chinese GB/T data reused in the file.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent — mandatory where required by NTON/DGCN conformity, MEM/INE or ENATREL/distributor review, lender specifications, or project-owner procurement; 60 Hz test-condition relevance should be confirmed
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or certification-scheme dependent under China GB/T inverter certification; not a Nicaragua EMC acceptance substitute

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC reports as supporting material only unless they explicitly map to IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 requirements and cover 60 Hz operation. For Nicaragua PV projects, prepare IEC EMC and harmonic evidence acceptable to the project reviewer, MEM/INE context, ENATREL or distributor process, and any NTON/DGCN conformity route that applies.

rows.requirement: Nicaragua Grid Connection — MEM / INE / ENATREL Review, IEC 62116, IEC 61727, and 60 Hz / 120-240 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied solar PV inverter projects in Nicaragua should be treated as project-level interconnection approvals involving MEM policy context, INE regulation, ENATREL transmission / system coordination where relevant, and the applicable distribution company, commonly DISNORTE/DISSUR. Nicaragua is an Americas-style 60 Hz market with low-voltage service commonly framed around 120/240 V, not a China-style 50 Hz / 220-380 V system. For inverter evidence, project reviewers commonly expect IEC-based documentation such as IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, validated with the firmware settings, trip thresholds, reconnection delays, and voltage/frequency windows intended for the Nicaraguan grid.
target_standards: MEM / INE electricity-sector oversight and project approval context for Nicaragua
ENATREL and relevant distributor interconnection requirements for grid-tied projects
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Nicaragua grid context — 60 Hz and 120/240 V low-voltage service; confirm project-specific voltage with ENATREL or the distributor
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power station connection, NB/T 32004 for grid-connected inverter technical specifications, GB/T 37408 for PV grid-connected inverter technical requirements, and domestic anti-islanding reports. These documents are based on China's 50 Hz grid and 220/380 V voltage baseline. They do not establish that the inverter firmware, protection relay settings, power-quality behavior, or anti-islanding response has been validated for Nicaragua's 60 Hz / 120-240 V environment.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: a Chinese 50 Hz grid-connection package is not Nicaragua grid-ready. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence at 60 Hz, IEC 61727 grid-interface evidence, a parameter-setting file for Nicaragua voltage/frequency windows, reconnection and trip settings, power-quality data, wiring and protection drawings, and commissioning procedures acceptable to the project engineer, ENATREL where applicable, and the distribution company. Where factory firmware was validated only for China, reconfiguration and retesting should be documented before interconnection submission.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for grid-tied projects — MEM/INE regulatory context and ENATREL or distributor interconnection acceptance are project gates; 60 Hz / 120-240 V settings must be confirmed for the actual connection point
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China domestic grid operator expectations but does not transfer to Nicaragua interconnection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Nicaragua's 60 Hz / 120-240 V grid is a real engineering gate for Chinese PV inverters validated at 50 Hz / 220-380 V. Treat Chinese GB/T and NB/T reports as background only; prepare 60 Hz IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence, Nicaragua-specific firmware settings, and ENATREL/distributor-ready interconnection documents before project submission.

rows.requirement: Nicaragua Distributed and Utility PV Project Gate — MEM Policy Context, INE Regulation, ENATREL / Distributor Coordination, and SIEPAC Context
rows.target_requirement: Nicaragua has a renewable-heavy electricity context including geothermal, wind, hydro, biomass, and growing solar deployment, and it participates in the Central American SIEPAC regional interconnection. For a grid-tied PV inverter, however, renewable policy support is not a substitute for equipment acceptance. Utility-scale and distributed projects require project-specific coordination with MEM/INE, ENATREL where transmission or system operation is involved, and DISNORTE/DISSUR or another applicable distributor at distribution level. Equipment documentation should identify the exact inverter model, grid-support functions, anti-islanding behavior, export-control functions if required by the project, and Spanish-language installation and commissioning instructions.
target_standards: MEM electricity-sector policy and planning role for Nicaragua
INE electricity regulation and supervision role for Nicaragua
ENATREL transmission and system coordination role where relevant
DISNORTE/DISSUR or other distributor project-level interconnection requirements
SIEPAC regional interconnection context for Central America
rows.china_equivalent: China distributed PV and utility-scale PV approvals are handled under Chinese energy policy, grid company connection procedures, and domestic standards such as NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408, and GB/T 19964. Those approvals are tied to Chinese grid codes, Chinese-language documentation, China grid voltage and frequency, and Chinese commissioning practice. They do not provide approval status with MEM, INE, ENATREL, or a Nicaraguan distributor.
china_standards: NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: there is no automatic transfer from a Chinese domestic PV approval package to a Nicaragua project approval package. Exporters should prepare Spanish-language technical files, model-family certificates, IEC safety and grid evidence, Nicaragua-specific settings, single-line diagrams, protection philosophy, commissioning records, and clear owner/operator responsibilities for MEM/INE and ENATREL or distributor review. SIEPAC participation may add regional coordination for large projects, but it does not remove the local interconnection gate.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a project approval matter for grid-tied PV — consult MEM/INE and the applicable ENATREL/distributor process before shipment or installation
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic distributed or utility PV approval is not transferable to Nicaragua

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Nicaragua's renewable-energy context and SIEPAC participation do not create automatic inverter acceptance. Prepare a Nicaragua-specific project file for MEM/INE and ENATREL or distributor review, including Spanish documentation, IEC evidence, and 60 Hz / 120-240 V firmware settings before committing a Chinese inverter model to a project.

source.publisher: Ministerio de Energía y Minas (MEM), Nicaragua

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — Nicaragua PV Inverter Project Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: For Nicaragua grid-tied PV projects, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the most appropriate international safety evidence for PV inverter power conversion equipment unless a project-specific NTON/DGCN, MEM/INE, ENATREL, distributor, lender, or owner specification identifies another accepted route. IEC 62109-1 covers general safety requirements for power converters in photovoltaic systems, while IEC 62109-2 covers particular inverter requirements. Safety evidence should cover the exact model or model family, input/output ratings, environmental ratings, protective devices, firmware-relevant safety functions, Spanish-language warnings and installation instructions, and the Nicaraguan 60 Hz grid configuration intended for use.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Nicaraguan NTON/DGCN conformity context where a national technical standard or project specification requires it
MEM / INE / ENATREL / distributor project requirements where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China's common PV inverter safety and performance file is based on GB/T 37408 and related domestic certification scheme requirements. GB/T 37408 includes safety and marking provisions and may be supported by CNAS laboratory reports. It is not, by itself, an IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificate and should not be treated as automatic evidence for Nicaragua unless the test report explicitly demonstrates IEC 62109 coverage for the same model family and configuration.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: a Chinese GB/T 37408 certificate does not automatically satisfy IEC 62109 safety expectations for a Nicaragua PV project. Exporters should obtain or compile IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates and reports from an accredited laboratory for the exact inverter model or family, confirm enclosure and environmental ratings for the installation site, supply Spanish-language safety documentation, and ensure the safety file aligns with the 60 Hz firmware and grid-connection configuration submitted for MEM/INE, ENATREL, distributor, or project-owner review.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly mandatory in practice for bankable grid-tied PV procurement, NTON/DGCN conformity paths, and MEM/INE or ENATREL/distributor review where IEC evidence is requested
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or certification-scheme dependent under China GB/T inverter certification; not a Nicaragua IEC 62109 substitute

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A PV inverter with only Chinese GB/T 37408 evidence should not be treated as Nicaragua-ready IEC 62109 evidence. Prepare IEC 62109-1/-2 model-family safety reports, Spanish installation and warning documentation, and a consistency check against the 60 Hz / 120-240 V configuration used for Nicaragua interconnection review.
